sourceMovie.title: Monsters, Inc.
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Monsters, Inc. (2001) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Minions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Monsters, Inc. clicked for you, Minions (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Simpsons Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Monsters, Inc. clicked for you, The Simpsons Movie (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Spirited Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Monsters, Inc. clicked for you, Spirited Away (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Monsters, Inc.?
faq.answer: Try Minions, The Simpsons Movie, The Lego Movie—each shares DNA with Monsters, Inc. while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Monsters, Inc.?
faq.answer: Monsters, Inc. (2001) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Monsters, Inc. part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Monsters, Inc. Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
